title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: Overall, it's quite playable. However, the issues I encountered were: the light effects on the fountains and portals flicker, the textures on the invisible walls sometimes don't render, and the framerate consistently drops to 1-10 fps after about two to three hours of gameplay on my system. All of these happened in Manjaro and Linux Mint, also. These issues are fairly minor, but I only tested single-player.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: Very few graphical bugs (perhaps 5 objects not rendered in 13 hours of gameplay, so I'm giving it a Platinum). No need to add anything for me, but I had to select the second item on the "play" list, since the first one wouldn't work. Good frame rates, fonts are bad but it seems to be a game problem.

title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: Game runs as fast as on Windows. Played through the whole storyline including Atlantis multiple times. There are some frame drops in the Atlantis part, but it's well playable. Things that don't work: multiplayer, Cerberus! Every time Cerberus used his poison rift attack the game crashed immediately. I was able to work around this issue by defeating him in DX9 mode and switching back to DX11 afterwards. However, first I had to get DX9 mode running correctly since the game would only show a black screen. Workaround: execute > WINEPREFIX=/home/user/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/475150/pfx/ "/home/user/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Proton 4.2/dist/bin/wine" winecfg < and set the Windows version for TQ.exe to Windows XP. Some fonts don't render correctly in DX9 mode, but since it's only a workaround for one boss, I don't care.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: Game works fine in general, but I encounted several texture glitches. There were crashes as well. Also severe FPS drop when using teleport.

title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes:
With proton 4-11-1 game works perfect after adding PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%

I was getting flickering using default launch option or black menu trying DX9. That command and the game works flawlessly on default launch option.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes:
Runs fine out of the box with DXVK.

Those who experience object flickering: disable HBAO, disable "detail objects" and reduce the level of texture quality from "high" until the flickering disappears.
IMPORTANT!!! Make the above settings while in the main menu. Otherwise the game would freeze and you'd have to reboot your system!!!
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes:
Thanks for all the other tips!
First try, I was get the black screen after launch the second option. First option crash instant. I try winecfg without success, BUT I FORGOT SOME realy important thing. I was installing Titan Quest on another drive. So make sure you use the correct path for run winecfg in a terminal-session. for example: 
WINEARCH=win64 WINEPREFIX='/media/..(your path)../SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/475150/pfx' winecfg. Now the game runs perfekt.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: Tested only singleplayer. Works fine out of the box with Proton 4.2-9. Before spent much time trying to play with Proton 4.11-6 and had game crashing at start at dx11 or missing textures on characters on dx9 (part of left hand on female character and part of right leg on male character and all npc's were not seen). With 4.2-9 that's all fixed.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: Had to run the game with DirectX 9 (second option running it), otherwise it does not launch. Changing graphics options sometimes stalls the game, but after a restart the new options are used. During gameplay everything worked as expected.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition

notes: I have played all the way to Act II, 83 hours in game. If I use the second launch option, which I think is DX9, I get black screen on startup. I have to use the first launch option to get it to work. Performance is excellent thanks to DVXK. HOWEVER, it also crashes regularly with DVXK errors in the console, and so far I have not found a solution to this other than to not use DVXK. Also, if I set focus away from game and back again, it will lock up or otherwise stop responding. I have not found any tweaks to get around this, therefore according to the rating system, I have to give this a SILVER rating. It is playable, but save often because it will crash often.
title: Titan Quest Anniversary Edition
